response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software engineer, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. The position requires native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX, Linux kernel source-level analysis, and embedded LAN-switch security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 networking). The resume lists English, Romanian, and German only; C/C++ experience is limited to a seven-month stint in 2014-2015; and there is no evidence of kernel work, networking protocols, or security technologies. The bulk of the career is web and full-stack development in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded-adjacent work
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second)
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and Linux/Docker/devops familiarity
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — the role requires native-level Japanese, a hard disqualifier
No experience with L2/L3 networking, switch products, or security technologies such as MACsec, 802.1X, or tamper detection
C for UNIX and Linux kernel modification experience is essentially absent; C/C++ exposure is brief and a decade old

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
Broad, senior general software engineering background
